title: Day 1 Forecast Discussion
discussion:
CESTOF SEVERE WEATHER DISCUSSION
Issued for: 4th May 2026
Area: France, Central Europe, Baltic States, Russia

HIGHEST RISK OF THE DAY: Slight Risk

The highest risk area includes: Central Czechia, West to North Poland, Lithuania, Latvia, Russia.

A Slight Risk means a few storms may become severe, especially where the best ingredients overlap.

RISK AREAS:
No risk-area list was entered.

METEOROLOGICAL DISCUSSION:

A severe-weather forecast has been issued for parts of France, Central Europe, Baltic States, Russia, mainly during Afternoon to Evening, as several convective ingredients are expected to overlap. The primary forcing mechanism should be a convergence zone may focus storm initiation where surface heating is strongest. In addition, forcing is moderate, supporting scattered initiation where instability is strongest, while no clear boundary is present, so storm initiation may be less focused. Early-day evolution may also play an important role, as clearer skies during the morning may allow stronger surface heating and destabilization.

Thermodynamically, dew points are modest, supporting thunderstorms but limiting higher-end instability, and moisture depth is moderate, supporting typical thunderstorm rainfall rates. At the same time, modest instability is present, with CAPE around 500–1000 J/kg, while very steep mid-level lapse rates are present, strongly supporting large hail potential if storms develop. This combination will determine how strong updrafts can become and whether storms are capable of producing hail or damaging wind gusts. Cloud bases also matter, as LCL heights are low, which may support better low-level stretching and a locally increased tornado potential. Meanwhile, CIN is quite strong and may suppress convection if forcing remains weak, meaning the final threat may depend strongly on whether storms can initiate in the best environment.

Kinematically, deep-layer shear is strong, around 25–35 m/s, supporting organized multicells and possible supercells. Near the surface, low-level shear is enhanced, supporting stronger low-level rotation if storms remain surface-based, and enhanced low-level helicity is present, supporting rotating storms and a conditional tornado risk. Furthermore, moderate upper-level support may help sustain convection and improve organization. These wind-field ingredients will control storm organization, rotation potential and the chance for storms to become long-lived.

Storm coverage is expected to be isolated, meaning storm coverage should remain isolated, so severe weather will likely be localized. In terms of movement, storm motion should be moderate, allowing storms to produce hazards without staying over one place for too long. Storm mode remains uncertain and will depend on initiation, boundaries and how quickly cells merge. Because of this, the final severe-weather threat will depend on how storms initiate and evolve. If storms remain discrete for a longer time, hail and rotation may become more relevant. If storms merge quickly into clusters or lines, damaging wind gusts and heavy rainfall will likely become the dominant hazards.

SETUP TYPE:

The setup is mixed, with some ingredients supporting stronger storms but uncertainty remaining in coverage, mode or intensity.

MAIN HAZARDS:

The main hazards are expected to be brief heavy rain, gusty winds and lightning. The most important hazard signals are possible discrete supercells.

LIMITING FACTORS:

No major limiting factors were selected.

FORECAST REASONING:

The forecast was issued because the environment contains enough ingredients for thunderstorms to become stronger or organized. Instability will provide energy for updrafts, while vertical wind shear will determine how organized and long-lived storms can become.

Where the best CAPE overlaps with stronger deep-layer shear, forcing and boundaries, storms may become more intense. If storms remain isolated, the threat for hail and possible rotation may be higher. If storms merge quickly into clusters or lines, the main threat will likely shift toward damaging wind gusts and heavy rainfall.

The final severity will depend on storm initiation. If forcing is strong enough and CIN is overcome, storms may develop rapidly. However, if cloud cover, weak lift or stronger inhibition remains in place, the overall threat may stay lower than the environment suggests.

CONFIDENCE:

Forecast confidence is low because storm initiation, placement or coverage remains uncertain.

SUMMARY:

Overall, Slight Risk is the highest risk for this forecast. The most important area is Central Czechia, West to North Poland, Lithuania, Latvia, Russia. Severe weather is not expected everywhere, but storms that form in the best environment may become strong to severe. The main threats are brief heavy rain, gusty winds and lightning.
